phase_name: CPU Training Core — Plain Boosting & Oblivious Trees

Compute boundary design.question: What should the CPU compute path be in Phase 3?
Compute boundary design.answer: CubeCL CPU runtime now — prove the CubeCL seam in Phase 3

Compute boundary design.question: How to coexist with the order-locked cb-core::sum_f64 invariant (D-08)?
Compute boundary design.answer: Kernels do order-independent elementwise work; every parity-critical SUM finalized host-side via cb-core::sum_f64 in frozen order

Compute boundary design.question: Where do the #[cube] kernels live and what is cb-compute's role?
Compute boundary design.answer: Kernels + cubecl dep ONLY in cb-backend; cb-compute defines own abstract R: Runtime/F: Float traits + host orchestration, never sees cubecl (D-03 honored)
options_presented: Split: traits in cb-compute, kernels in cb-backend, share cubecl::Float

Compute boundary design.question: Trait granularity?
Compute boundary design.answer: Coarse domain ops (build_histograms, compute_gradients, eval_splits); backend owns kernel decomposition, host finalizes ordered sums

Threading & determinism.question: How to make per-bin histogram accumulation deterministic vs thread_count=1?
Threading & determinism.answer: Ordered single-pass f64 per-bin accumulators in canonical object order (cb-core::sum_f64 semantics); kernel computes per-object gradients in parallel

Threading & determinism.question: Phase 3 threading target?
Threading & determinism.answer: Parallel elementwise kernels now (order-independent gradient/hessian), all reductions ordered host-side — exercises CubeCL parallelism early, de-risks Phase 7

First oracle-locked slice.question: Parameter posture for the first slice?
First oracle-locked slice.answer: Simplified isolating params (bootstrap_type=No, random_strength=0, fixed l2/depth/lr/iterations); each knob added as its own additive oracle

First oracle-locked slice.question: Anchor loss?
First oracle-locked slice.answer: Both RMSE + Logloss in the first slice (regression + binary classification together)

First oracle-locked slice.question: Leaf-method rollout?
First oracle-locked slice.answer: All four (Gradient/Newton/Exact/Simple) up front — complete TRAIN-03 in one wave

Intermediate-oracle depth.question: How deep should the oracle go?
Intermediate-oracle depth.answer: Python-reachable floor: per-tree splits + leaf values + per-iteration approximants (save_model json + staged_predict); no C++ instrumentation (honors P1 D-08); drift localized by the simplified first slice
